title: One dead, four injured in IED blast close to Assam Rifles vehicle in Nagaland: Police official
content: The explosion reportedly targeted an Assam Rifles vehicles during an operational movement, PRO Defence Col Amit Shukla said

title: Security forces launch search operations in Manipur after two Assam Rifles personnel killed
content: On July 10, Assam Rifles said the paramilitary force, in coordination with the Border Security Force and Manipur Police, commenced a large-scale search operation in the Nungshang area

title: 20 booked for chaos at Adoor police station
content: The accused, which included Youth Congress workers, allegedly attacked Mr. Joseph as well as the officers while the Youtuber was being escorted to the station

title: BJD moves child rights panel over alleged use of children at BJP political rally in Odisha
content: The regional party demands notices be issued against Odisha CM Mohan Charan Majhi, State BJP president Manmohan Samal and former party MLA Pravat Biswal

title: Polavaram, Cotton Barrage renovation will be ready before Godavari Pushkarams: Naidu
content: Performing bhumi puja for the replacement of 117 barrage gates, the Chief Minister said 89% of Polavaram was complete and the project would be dedicated to the nation by March 2027

title: TTD board member objects to Shivakumar’s ‘Tirumala Nitya Harathi’ offer to Karnataka VIPs
content: G. Bhanuprakash Reddy called the Karnataka CM’s announcement an ‘authoritative overreach’ made without consulting the TTD, and said the issue would go before the trust board on Tuesday

title: Former MP Prajwal Revanna challenges conviction in rape case in Karnataka High Court
content: Revanna, who was arrested in May last year after returning from Germany, is contesting the verdict on several grounds, including what he claims are contradictions in the survivor's testimony and inconsistencies in the evidence produced by the prosecution

title: Bengaluru special court discharges H.D. Revanna from sexual harassment case citing unexplained delay of over four years in lodging complaint
content: Indicating that the complainant may have forgotten the alleged incidents involving Mr. Revanna as memory fades if the offences are not severe in nature, the Court said that subsequent serious offences of alleged rape by Prajwal Revanna might have, as an afterthought, made the survivor complain against Mr. Revanna.

title: Piduguralla Government Medical College gets NMC nod for 100 MBBS seats
content: College to admit its first batch of students from the 2026-27 academic year, strengthening medical education and healthcare in Palnadu district

title: ‘We tried CPR, but it was too late,’ say Vietnam boat tragedy witnesses on return to Hyderabad
content: Several Indian tourists who were stranded in Vietnam after the tragedy landed in Hyderabad on the night of July 12

title: Sahitya Akademi Award-winning Tamil writer Poomani passes away
content: Poomani received the Sahitya Akademi Award in 2014 for his monumental novel Agnaadi, an epic narrative set against the backdrop of the Sivakasi (1899) and Kazhugumalai (1895) communal riots; Chief Minister Vijay announces State honours

title: Retail inflation inches up to 4.38% in June: Government data
content: The food inflation based on the consumer price index (CPI) was 5.32% in June, higher than 4.78% in May, according to the data

title: How El Niño could damage India’s economy | Explained
content: A poor monsoon can damage the economy in three ways: it affects agricultural output, reducing the sector’s contribution to the economy; it hits rural income, denting aggregate demand; and it threatens to push up food prices, causing inflation.

title: Uproar in Chhattisgarh Assembly over Ram Temple donation theft issue
content: People’s faith and trust have been betrayed, says Opposition; Treasury benches argue the matter is outside the scope of the Assembly’s business.

title: Ram Temple donation row: RSS expresses concern over ‘misuse of funds’, voices confidence in SIT probe
content: Delegates, at the regional convenors’ meeting in Belagavi, underscored the need for measures that ensure that such incidents do not recur

title: Real culprits being protected in Ram Temple donation embezzlement case: Congress MP
content: Senior Congress leader Pramod Tiwari reiterates demand for a Supreme Court-monitored probe into the matter

title: Indian Railways registers growth in freight, passenger services
content: The Railways loaded 142.21 million tonnes of freight during June 2026, compared with 136.71 million tonnes in June 2025; freight earnings generated an additional revenue of about ₹430 crore

title: Malayali youngsters develop rail hygiene kit addressing sanitary concerns over long distance train travel
content: Rail hygiene kit by Travel Essentia, a startup founded by four Malayali youngsters, consists of 10 components for maintaining personal hygiene

title: SC asks Centre to place on record new aviation rules to regulate volatile airfares
content: New regulations have been finalised, the government says, they are being translated into Hindi and other official languages before being tabled in Parliament

title: Lok Sabha members ask for airfare regulation mechanism
content: ‘Cutting across party lines, MPs demand an authority, which could be a quasi-judicial body on the lines of SEBI, to look into issues related to unregulated airfares, including ‘cartelisation’

title: Airfares need to be ‘rationalised’, says Supreme Court
content: Centre, in an affidavit, informs top court that new aviation rules addressing predatory airfares are at an “advanced stage” of formulation

title: SC seeks H.D. Revanna’s response on Karnataka’s plea against High Court order quashing the charge of outraging modesty
content: Apex court expresses reservations over the High Court’s interference after the trial court had already taken cognisance of the offence; Bench also questions Karnataka government over delay in challenging High Court’s order

title: India-Japan relations should not target third party: China
content: India and Japan also expressed "serious concern" at the situation in the East China Sea and South China Sea, and opposed unilateral actions that endanger freedom of navigation as well as attempts to change the status quo by force

title: Kerala government not seeking confrontation with judiciary, says Minister K. Muraleedharan
content: Dewaswom Minister says government needs greater freedom to function, particularly in matters relating to temple administration; he adds that  court can intervene if there are flaws in government decisions

title: Karnataka Governor suspends KPSC chairperson Shivashankarappa Sahukar over 'illegal selection' of daughter in government service
content: The Governor recommended to the President that the matter be referred to the Supreme Court under Article 317(1) of the Constitution for an inquiry into the allegations

title: Day 24 of CJP protest: Wangchuk loses 8.2 kg as fast enters 16th day; 'lives at stake', says Dipke
content: On the day, an AAP delegation led by former Delhi Chief Minister Atishi visited the protest site and expressed solidarity with the agitation, according to the CJP

title: Karnataka gender minority and intersex community representatives demand welfare board, ordinance against Trans Act, 2026
content: The delegation also requested the State Government to promulgate an ordinance in the Karnataka Legislative Assembly against the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Act, 2026, recently passed by the Central Government.

title: Railway Board seeks zones' inputs on CAG findings on dirty toilets, coach cleanliness
content: The Railway Board has sought a detailed response from all the zones to address observations made by the Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG)

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: India, Japan hold key dialogue to boost ties in defence industrial collaboration, cyber security
content: India and Japan held their eighth Defence Policy Dialogue in Tokyo on Monday (July 13, 2026), India's Defence Ministry said in a statement

title: Kozhikode police widen net in probe into narco-mule network
content: Law enforcement agencies identify suspicious mule accounts, which are personal bank accounts leased out by individuals to receive illegal transactions on commission basis; narcotics kingpins found exploiting youngsters to act as financial proxies

title: Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini directs portal for scientific disposal of end-of-life vehicles
content: The State should make optimum use of modern technology to promote clean air, green transport and sustainable development says Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini

title: One hospitalised, two suffer breathing difficulties after Mumbai AC local cooling system fails
content: According to Railway officials, the incident occurred during peak hours at around 9.26 a.m. when the AC-system of the 8.33 a.m. Titwala-CSMT local developed a technical error, shutting down the cooling system

title: Calcutta High Court to hear TMC Mamata Banerjee-led faction's petition for permission for rally on July 15
content: On July 10, a petiton was filed by Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool Congress faction to seek direction to the police to allow its July 21 Martyrs' Day rally in front of Victoria House at Esplanade

title: Modi government silent on corruption in education; time for revolution in this sector: Rahul Gandhi
content: LoP Rahul Gandhi alleged that in the face of corruption, the Modi government and Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan have chosen silence, turning their face away from accountability

title: Mizoram Government urges tourists to defer travel as heavy rain causes inundation, landslips
content: Around 200 travellers remained stranded at Bualte village in Lunglei due to a massive landslide on the National Highway 54 that links Lawngtlai and Siaha districts

title: Which States have banned cow slaughter in India?
content: The slaughter, processing, and transport of bovine animals is governed by differing state legislation.

title: ‘An assault on collective memory’: Sukhbir Badal slams removal of Satluj from  OTT platform
content: “A powerful film that courageously unveils Punjab’s painful history and honours the supreme sacrifice of S. Jaswant Singh Ji Khalra cannot be silenced this way,” Sukhbir Singh Badal said

title: Legal questions behind the row over Satluj movie | Explained
content: Can the government legally order an OTT platform to remove a film? What are the legal safeguards? Can the blocking order remain confidential? What are the legal remedies?

title: Amid 'Satluj' row, Khalra's wife Paramjit Kaur urges Akal Takht to form 'People's Commission'
content: “The military attack on Sri Darbar Sahib [Golden Temple] in June 1984, the Sikh genocide in November 1984, and the unidentified bodies, torture, and thousands of fake police encounters in the years that followed still demand accountability and justice,”says Paramjit Kaur Khalra

title: Punjab Governor gave nod to ordinance on capping fees by private schools: CM
content: The Punjab Cabinet had last month approved an ordinance on capping fee hikes by private schools at 5% per annum; education has regrettably been made a business in the State, says CM Bhagwant Mann

title: Educationist Ishari K. Ganesh withdraws plea in Madras High Court to quash money laundering case
content: Madras High Court permits him to withdraw the plea with liberty to approach the court as and when an occasion arises

title: Retrenchments in Kerala: Labour codes and its rules | Explained
content: Chapter XI of the Code provides that the appropriate government shall set up a worker re-skilling fund, and the employer must contribute to the fund an amount equal to 15 days’ wages last drawn by the worker immediately before the retrenchment

title: Centre brings amnesty scheme for PF Trusts to regularise their status
content: Recognition under the Income Tax Act, 2025 shall be available only to provident funds that have obtained exemption under Section 17 of the 1952 Act

title: Right of Way: The right to walk safely demands more than urban design standards for demarcated pavements
content: The Supreme Court’s  judgment offers an opportunity to re-evaluate standards that only partially align with the cities they were meant to serve

title: Watch: Iran 'completely destroys' Patriot air defences, fuel tanks, radar at US Gulf bases
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guard claimed missile and drone strikes on US bases in three countries. These operations targeted air defense systems and fuel depots in retaliation. The IRGC stated the attacks were a response to recent American military strikes. US Central Command confirmed precision strikes against Iranian military installations. Both sides engaged in escalating military actions in the region.

title: Teen dies as SUV driven by minor hits scooter, crashes into school bus
content: A 17-year-old boy died after a speeding car hit his scooter. The car then crashed into a school mini-bus on Warehouse Road. The teenager was critically injured and later succumbed to his injuries. Eyewitnesses reported the car driver and occupants fled the scene. Police are searching for the absconding driver and scanning CCTV footage.

title: India's 40-year-old streak in England continues with historic Lord's win
content: India’s historic 270-run win over England in the first women’s Test at Lord’s extended their unbeaten Test record in England to 40 years. Yastika Bhatia’s landmark century and Kranti Gaud’s seven-wicket match haul powered the victory. India now lead the overall head-to-head 4-1, with 11 draws, underlining their growing dominance in women’s Test cricket.

title: Watch: Puja inside moving train coach stuns social media; Railway replies
content: A viral video showed a priest performing rituals inside a moving train coach. Northern Railway clarified the ceremony occurred in a privately booked saloon car. This luxury coach was commercially booked through IRCTC for a specific journey. The railway stated no passengers were inconvenienced by the private booking. The incident followed another video of a decorated train coach causing outrage.

title: Instagram, Facebook hiding a 'dark secret,' EU claims; company could pay a huge fine
content: The European Commission has preliminarily found Meta in breach of the Digital Services Act, ruling Instagram and Facebook's addictive design—infinite scroll, autoplay, personalised feeds—harms users, especially teens. Brussels wants autoplay and infinite scroll disabled by default, plus real screen time breaks. Meta faces a fine up to 6% of global turnover and disputes the findings, citing its Teen Accounts safeguards as adequate protection already in place.

title: Emotional after Datia ticket snub, Narottam Mishra chokes up at BJP rally
content: Senior BJP leader Narottam Mishra campaigned emotionally for Ashutosh Tiwari's nomination. He pledged to personally campaign in every village for the upcoming by-election. Mishra defended the BJP government's development record and attacked the Congress party. He asserted there was no internal discord within the BJP regarding the candidate. Ashutosh Tiwari thanked the leadership and expressed confidence in meeting expectations.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
content: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the nonprofit's history. Active Minds focuses on youth mental health awareness and reducing stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support young leaders. This donation occurs amid growing concerns about youth mental health.

title: Uddhav extends support to CJP protest as Wangchuk's hunger strike enters day 16
content: Uddhav Thackeray has voiced support for the Cockroach Janta Party's protest. He backs climate activist Sonam Wangchuk and CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke's demands. Thackeray urged political parties and citizens to unite for the youth's future. He criticized the government's insensitivity to genuine concerns raised. Thackeray's party MPs will also raise the NEET issue in Parliament.

title: Dubai Sheikh visited huge Scotland estate only 5 times in 20 years
content: In a surprising move, Dubai's ruler enhanced his Scottish estate by adding ten lavish properties complete with helipads. His representatives suggest that the ruler's limited visits stem from a lack of adequate accommodation for his extensive entourage. Local residents voiced their objections, citing concerns over privacy and the visual impact of the developments. While the estate has become a lively hub for private jets, its primary owner seldom visits.

title: Iowa mom’s simple garage game for sons becomes a 28M-view hit
content: An Iowa mother, Megan, is turning her driveway into a canvas with creative water art projects that have captured the internet's attention. Using just a garden hose and various garage items, she collaborates with her sons, Carter and Parker, to craft eye-catching designs including sports logos. Their imaginative summer endeavors have racked up millions of views, as Megan hopes to motivate families to create unforgettable moments with easy home crafts.

title: Satya Nadella warns AI users: You are paying for your own IP, suggests 5 solutions
content: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella says enterprises using AI are paying twice—once in cash, once in proprietary knowledge. In a post viewed 5.7 million times, he calls it the "Reverse Information Paradox": every prompt, eval and correction leaks institutional know-how to model providers. He flags the irony of fair use for vendors, restrictive terms for buyers, and offers a five-point fix: Control, Capability, Choice, Cost, Compound.

title: 2020 Delhi riots: Court convicts Tahir Hussain in IB officer Ankit Sharma murder case
content: A Delhi court convicted former AAP councillor Tahir Hussain and others for murder. The court found Hussain guilty of murder and rioting charges related to the 2020 riots. Nazim, Kashim, Anas, and Javed were also convicted for murder and other offenses. The court acquitted six other individuals accused in the case. Arguments on the quantum of sentence will be heard later.

title: Woman thrashed with sticks in front of police, dies
content: In a shocking incident in Chhatarpur, a 55-year-old woman lost her life following a savage attack by several women, allegedly stemming from an old rivalry linked to a romantic affair. Disturbingly, a viral clip reveals the attackers taking breaks for water amid their prolonged assault. The local police are delving into the case, facing criticism for perceived inaction from a Dial-112 response team.

title: Yastika, Kranti inspire India's historic Lord's Test win over England
content: India registered a historic 270-run victory over England in the first-ever women’s Test at Lord’s. Yastika Bhatia’s landmark 113, Richa Ghosh’s unbeaten fifty and Smriti Mandhana’s 70 helped set a daunting 457-run target. Kranti Gaud’s seven-wicket match haul and disciplined bowling from Sneh Rana and Sayali Satghare ensured India sealed a commanding, all-round triumph.

title: NHL Trade Rumors: Penguins linked to $92.8M Western Conference forward
content: Elias Pettersson's trade speculation is gaining real traction after Elliotte Friedman named the Pittsburgh Penguins as a potential destination on his 32 Thoughts podcast. With Vancouver finishing last in the West and a new leadership group publicly pressing Pettersson to raise his standards, the Canucks forward's future is far from settled. His $11.6 million cap hit and player control make any move complicated, but with Pittsburgh seeking one more competitive push alongside Crosby and Malkin, the fit has at least caught the attention of one of the sport's most plugged-in insiders.

title: Drone vs drone: How interceptors are changing the cost equation of air defence
content: Countries are investing in drone countermeasures due to their battlefield prevalence. Ukraine developed interceptor drones to counter cheaper hostile systems effectively. These interceptor drones offer flexibility and can be reused after missions. India is also developing similar drone interceptor systems for defense. These new systems provide a cost-effective solution against drone threats.

title: 'Self-immolation, squatter evictions': Why Nepal's Gen Z protesters are back on the streets
content: Public fury is mounting against Nepal's Prime Minister Balen Shah, fueled by ongoing protests. The self-immolation of a ride-sharing worker has spurred vigorous youth-led demonstrations demanding accountability for government policies. Thousands have assembled, charging the administration with overlooking the struggles of its most vulnerable citizens. The recent squatter evictions have further catalyzed calls for human rights safeguards. Following these events, the government has initiated a probe into the tragic occurrence.

title: Pro-Khalistan group issues bomb threat ahead of PM Modi's Haryana visit; security upped
content: Security agencies are on high alert following a pro-Khalistan group's email threat. The group claims to target Prime Minister Modi's rally in Jind, Haryana. Bomb blasts are also threatened at schools and railway stations on July 17. Union minister Ravneet Singh Bittu also received a threat from the group. Authorities are investigating the email's authenticity and assessing the threat's credibility.

title: 'God is a better writer': Harmanpreet Kaur reflects on India's historic Lord's triumph
content: India secured a historic 270-run victory against England at Lord's. Captain Harmanpreet Kaur praised the team's resilience and support staff's collective effort. Openers Smriti Mandhana and Shafali Verma provided a strong foundation for the win. Yastika Bhatia's century and the team's fielding were also highlighted. This memorable performance followed a disappointing T20 World Cup campaign.

title: Wrong'un! India's once-feared spin arsenal is running dry
content: India's once-formidable spin attack is losing its edge. From repeated failures to take wickets in the middle overs against Ireland, England and in recent T20I series, to a thinning talent pipeline, India's traditional strength has become a growing concern, forcing the team management to increasingly rely on pace rather than spin.

title: Is your dream home affordable? 33% rule every buyer should know about
content: Rising housing costs in India are straining household budgets significantly. A recent study has revealed the stark difficulties individuals face in finding affordable housing. The 33% rule advises that no more than a third of one's monthly income should go towards housing expenses. While affordability calculators provide insight into borrowing potential, banks often evaluate additional factors before granting home loan approvals.

title: White gold to memory: How Goa's 1,500-year-old salt pans are vanishing
content: Goa's centuries-old salt industry is now on the brink of collapse.  Many salt pans have disappeared, leaving only a few operational today.  Real estate pressure and competition from refined salt have severely impacted this heritage trade.  Despite challenges, some efforts are underway to revive and preserve this unique cultural landscape.  Without government support and land protection, this traditional craft may soon vanish forever.

title: China close to cracking code of tech that controls the world - all about EUV machine
content: China has reportedly developed a functional prototype of an EUV lithography system. This development challenges Western assumptions about China's chip manufacturing capabilities. The system utilizes a mix of indigenous breakthroughs and shadow procurement networks. Experts suggest mass production could be achieved by 2030. This marks a significant shift in the global semiconductor landscape.

title: 'Real' TMC row: Mamata flags EC's 'stoic silence', opposes more time for rebel camp
content: Mamata Banerjee asked the Election Commission to stop granting more time to the Ritabrata Banerjee faction. She alleged the poll body's silence gave the rebel group additional leeway. The rival faction claimed to be the real Trinamool Congress party. Mamata's camp argued its organizational committees remain valid until 2027. The Election Commission had previously set deadlines for both groups.

title: Inside Tom Holland's $4.7 M property in London: Gym, cinema, 'man cave' & more
content: During his recent trip to Mumbai for film promotions, Hollywood star Tom Holland revealed his London home, which he bought in 2018, has seen major renovations. The upgrades have significantly boosted the property's value, while the home now boasts a private gym and a luxurious home cinema. Holland values his privacy, ensuring little is known about his personal life and living arrangements.

title: Retail inflation jumps to 4.38% in June; breaches RBI's 4% target
content: India's retail inflation increased to 4.38 percent in June from May's 3.93 percent. Higher food prices significantly pushed up the overall Consumer Price Index for the month. Ginger and tomatoes saw substantial year-on-year price increases, contributing to the overall rise. However, some items like potatoes and peas became cheaper compared to last year. Rural areas experienced higher inflation rates than urban centers during this period.

title: Commission orders RedBus to pay Rs 10K for cancelling ticket 17 minutes before departure
content: A consumer commission found RedBus and Bharmani Travels jointly responsible for a cancelled ticket. The passenger was left stranded late at night after his confirmed booking was cancelled. RedBus argued it was merely an intermediary, but the commission disagreed with this stance. The commission ordered both parties to pay compensation for harassment and losses. This ruling emphasizes platform accountability even after accepting payments and confirming bookings.

title: Watch: Woman slaps man sexually harassing her in MP bus
content: A man was detained after a video showed him making gestures on a bus. The viral footage sparked outrage and concerns over women's safety. Police are investigating the incident and verifying video authenticity. No formal complaint has been filed, so no FIR is registered. Strict legal action will follow if a cognizable offense is established.

title: 5 deadlines missed since 2020: Why Delhi’s landfill crisis continues
content: Delhi's landfill flattening deadlines are repeatedly missed by the MCD. Fresh waste continues to be dumped daily, hindering progress on legacy waste. Bio-mining targets are being exceeded at some sites, while others lag behind. New waste-to-energy plants are planned, but will take years to become operational. Residents face ongoing health and environmental challenges from the garbage mountains.

title: 'We rushed Sooryavanshi': Ex-India batter says team got carried away by hype
content: Former India batter Wasim Jaffer believes Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's debut was premature. He felt public excitement influenced the decision to play the young player. Jaffer suggested Sooryavanshi needed more time with the national team setup. Sanju Samson's subsequent omission from the Zimbabwe tour also surprised Jaffer. Yuvraj Singh, however, expressed strong confidence in Sooryavanshi's future.

title: Anupam Kher's response to Naseeruddin calling him a clown goes viral: Watch
content: Naseeruddin Shah's past remarks calling Anupam Kher a "clown" have resurfaced online. This occurred after Kher's recent comments on the Ayodhya Ram Temple donation theft. Kher had dismissed the reported theft as a small issue compared to historical temple destructions. Shah's 2020 interview, where he criticized Kher's political views, is now widely shared. Kher had previously responded to Shah's criticism, accusing him of frustration and substance abuse.

title: ITR filing: 10 penalties taxpayers should not ignore
content: ITR filing: What are the various sections under which income tax penalties are applicable? When does a default happen and what are the penalty amounts that have to be paid by taxpayers? Here is a list of 10 top 10 penalties that can be imposed:

title: Same Soviet DNA, different fighters: How India's Su-30MKI compares with China's J-16
content: The Su-30MKI from India and China's J-16 fighters are both rooted in similar design principles but show distinct differences. The Su-30MKI offers superior agility thanks to its thrust vectoring and canard configuration. In contrast, the J-16 boasts sophisticated indigenous radar and electronic warfare technology. While the J-16 excels in beyond-visual-range engagements, India is focusing on upgrades to bolster the Su-30MKI's operational capabilities.

title: TG TET 2026 result declared: Check direct link to download scorecards
content: The Department of School Education, Telangana, has announced the TG TET 2026 results and activated the marks memo download facility on the official portal. Candidates can check their scores using their login credentials and save their scorecards for future use. The qualification is essential for candidates seeking teaching opportunities in government and aided schools across the state.

title: Patna firing case: 'Khan Sir', 3 coaching institute staffers get anticipatory bail
content: Patna court granted anticipatory bail to educator Khan Sir and three staff members. The case involves a firing incident at Khan Global Studies Institute in early June. Two security guards also received bail after being arrested in connection with the incident. Investigation revealed the guards fired in the air from licensed arms in self-defense. Khan Sir had alleged a rival orchestrated the vandalism and firing at his institute.

title: USS Columbia: The US nuclear submarine built to run 42 years without reactor refueling
content: The United States is in the process of building its advanced Columbia-class submarines, designed to strengthen its nuclear deterrent. These submarines will incorporate a life-of-ship nuclear reactor, which will eliminate the need for mid-life refueling, enhancing operational readiness and lowering long-term costs. Set to replace the older Ohio-class vessels, the first of these submarines is expected to be delivered in 2029, greatly improving stealth capabilities and survivability at sea.

title: "Completely False": Piyush Goyal On Report India Rejected Quick US Trade Deal
content: Union Minister Piyush Goyal in the post on X said he had "fantastic meetings with USTR Jamieson Greer when he visited Delhi in June"

title: Supreme Court Seeks Centre's Airfare Regulation Rules In Sealed Cover Within 2 Weeks
content: The Centre told the court that rules to regulate airfare pricing have already been framed and are expected to be laid before Parliament within 30 days

title: 4 Techies Die After Car Rams Truck. They Were Driving To Jim Corbett Park
content: Twelve people had set out from Delhi for a company holiday to Jim Corbett Park in Ramnagar, Uttarakhand, travelling in two vehicles.

title: Past, Present And Future Of Rainwater Harvesting In India
content: India has roughly 18 per cent of the world's population but controls only 4 per cent of global freshwater resources. Projects like the Jal Shakti Abhiyan are working towards conserving rainwater. But there is still room for improvement.

title: IndiGo Aircraft Engineer Arrested With Heroin At Leh Airport
content: During routine patrolling in the city, a team of the Anti-Narcotics Task Force intercepted a vehicle and recovered 2.27 grams of heroin from the accused's possession.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Sharad Pawar Has An Old 'Two Plus Two' Bargain For Delhi
content: Sharad Pawar appears to be keeping two doors open, and the political cost could be huge for both opposition and the NDA.

title: Ahead Of Rath Yatra, Bengal Announces Rs 5 Lakh Aid To 60 Committees
content: The state government will also make special arrangements for Kanwar pilgrims during this year's Shravan Mela, with Suvendu Adhikari visiting Tarakeswar on July 14.

title: Why Nepal's GenZ Is Again On Streets Against PM They Elected Just Months Ago
content: On Sunday, hundreds of people took to the streets outside the Singhdurbar Secretariat, a government office in the capital. Many of them carried placards with slogans like 'End atrocity against the poor' and 'Respect human rights.'

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex, Nifty Settle Flat As US-Iran Tensions Escalate
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Crude oil prices jump 3% amid renewed US-Iran war tensions; Brent crude above $78 per barrel.

title: Chhattisgarh Engineer Kills Girlfriend In Gurugram, Jumps In Front Of A Train
content: Shreshth Malik, 25, died by suicide by jumping in front of a train after allegedly stabbed his girlfriend to death.

title: Karnataka Governor Suspends KPSC Chief Over Daughters' Selection Row
content: Karnataka Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot suspended KPSC Chairman Sahukar over allegations he helped his daughters get illegally selected as officers

title: Centre Reviews WhatsApp, Telegram Replies On Usernames, Uniform Rules Likely
content: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) had earlier issued notices to WhatsApp, Telegram and Signal after raising concerns that username-based messaging

title: After Phone Goes Missing, National Award-Winning Farmer Loses Rs 83,000
content: Despite running from pillar to post for over three and a half months, approaching the police station, the Cyber Crime Department, and bank authorities, he has neither recovered the money nor received any resolution.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: US, Iran Both Claim Control Of Strait Of Hormuz As Tensions Escalate
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: The flare-up is the latest to undermine an interim agreement between Washington and Tehran aimed at ending their war, which has caused global economic shockwaves since it began in late February.

title: CBI Files Report, Rules Out Criminal Negligence By Officials In 2024 Coaching Centre Deaths
content: CBI's supplementary report finds negligence by 2 more MCD officials in Rau's IAS basement deaths but clears senior officers, no chargesheet filed

title: Telangana Farmers Hold 'Frog Ceremony' For Rain Amid Monsoon Deficit
content: From June 1 to July 10, Telangana recorded a seasonal rainfall deficit of about 19%, with many districts experiencing deficient rainfall.

title: Watch: Russian live-fire exercise goes wrong as soldier loses control of machine gun
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: India's forex reserves explained: What they are, who owns them & how RBI uses them
content: the forex kitty had expanded to an all-time high of $728.494 billion during the week ended February 27 this year . the rupee came under pressure and the RBI had to intervene in the forex market through dollar sales . prime minister Narendra Modi has also appealed to countrymen to conserve forex by cutting down on foreign travel .
